summary: Split the large game-mode translation unit gm_1A36.c into address-bounded units gm_1A36.c, gm_1A3F.c, gm_1A45.c, and gm_1A4C.c, with matching config, symbols, headers, and static-header reshuffling. The decomp.dev report recorded +3312 matched bytes and +0.09% matched code for GALE01, including 50 new matches in the new split units; the reported broken matches/regressions in gm_1A36 are largely the expected consequence of moving functions into the new objects. The PR also tightened related types and prototypes, notably MinorSceneInfo, callback argument types, GameState::data as a function pointer, lb_80019880 as a u64 setter, __cvt_dbl_usll as returning u64 and taking double, and SObjLib declarations needed by the moved gm setup code.
what_changed: Changed GALE01 splits so the old gm_1A36.c text range 0x801A3680-0x801AEE6C became four objects: gm_1A36.c ending at 0x801A3F48, gm_1A3F.c covering 0x801A3F48-0x801A45E8, gm_1A45.c covering 0x801A45E8-0x801A4CE0, and gm_1A4C.c covering 0x801A4CE0-0x801AEE6C, with corresponding data/bss/sdata/sbss/sdata2 ranges.
Added new build objects in configure.py for melee/gm/gm_1A3F.c, gm_1A45.c, and gm_1A4C.c as NonMatching units.
Moved a large body of code out of gm_1A36.c into new source files and reduced gm_1A36.c to the first segment plus its local includes.
Added headers and static headers for the new split units, using address-commented prototypes and local static declarations.
Refined symbol boundaries in config/GALE01/symbols.txt, splitting oversized data/string labels such as lbl_803D7910 and gm_803DD4D0 into smaller objects and newly identified string labels.
Introduced MinorSceneInfo inside struct MinorScene, grouping class_id, unk_struct_0, and unk_struct_1, and updated code to pass &var_r27->info to scene-handler logic.
Changed MinorSceneHandler callback prototypes so OnLoad and OnLeave take void* rather than u32, matching their use with MinorSceneInfo data pointers.
Changed GameState::data from void* to u8 (*)(void), matching calls that compute a pending major scene id.
Corrected lb_80019880 from a two-s32 signature to a single u64 argument and made lb_804329F0_t store a u64 at offset 0x08.
Corrected __cvt_dbl_usll in Runtime to have signature ASM u64 __cvt_dbl_usll(double x), with the header updated accordingly.
Added SObjLib declarations for HSD_SObjLib_8040C3A4, HSD_SObjLib_804D7960, and HSD_SObjLib_803A44A4 to support gm_801A4BD4.
Added comments in mnstagesel.c identifying mnStageSel_8025A998 as OnLoad and mnStageSel_8025B850 as OnFrame.
smart_moves: The split was aligned exactly to address boundaries and accompanied by section range updates, making the move visible to the linker and to decomp.dev instead of treating it as a pure source shuffle.
The PR updated symbols.txt at the same time as the split, avoiding stale object sizes where contiguous data actually contained separate strings or tables.
Grouping class_id and the two minor-scene data pointers into MinorSceneInfo made callback plumbing more type-directed and removed several raw field accesses.
Changing callback and function-pointer prototypes to match actual use reduced casts and helped newly moved functions match in their proper units.
The author kept unresolved functions as address-comment stubs while extracting the matched and partially matched code, preserving future work anchors after the split.
decomp_lessons: When splitting a large TU, expect decomp.dev to report broken matches in the old unit and new matches in the new units; interpret the net matched-byte and new-unit reports rather than the old-unit regressions alone.

Section ranges in splits.txt must be split for every relevant section, not just .text; gm_1A45.c needed data, bss, sdata, sbss, and sdata2 ranges, while gm_1A4C.c inherited later rodata/data/bss ranges.
Data-symbol granularity matters for matching: oversized 4byte/string labels can block clean splits and should be carved into precise objects when evidence supports it.
Nested structs can encode discovered layout relationships without changing total size; MinorSceneInfo preserved MinorScene semantics while making handler code clearer.
Callback signatures should be driven by call sites and data flow; OnLoad/OnLeave used pointer payloads, not integer payloads.
Runtime/library prototype corrections can be necessary for seemingly unrelated gm matches because code generation depends on return type, argument type, and 64-bit value handling.
Adding missing baselib externs is preferable to local ad hoc externs when a split exposes library dependencies across files.
naming_conventions: New gm split files were named by their starting address prefix: gm_1A3F.c, gm_1A45.c, and gm_1A4C.c.
Public headers follow the source name, e.g. gm_1A3F.h and gm_1A45.h; local declarations use .static.h.
Function prototypes retain address comments such as /* 1A43A0 */ u8 gm_801A43A0(u8 arg0).
Unknown global symbols retain address-derived names such as gm_80479D30, gm_80479D58, and gm_803DA888 until better names are justified.
Scene callbacks are named/described as OnLoad, OnFrame, OnLeave, Prep, Decide, Load, Unload, and Init where structure evidence supports it.
The new MinorSceneInfo type names the grouped minor-scene payload instead of leaving class_id and payload pointers as loose MinorScene fields.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used inline helper functions matchMinor, getScene, and findSceneMatching to reproduce control-flow/codegen patterns in gm_1A3F.c.
Kept M2C_FIELD for fields whose structure names or exact layout were not yet resolved, such as gm_80479D30 byte access and gm_80479D58 offsets.
Used a u64 table and 1ULL bit operations in gm_1A45.c for bitmask-producing functions such as gm_801A48A4 and flag tests/sets.
Inserted PAD_STACK(0x18) in gm_801A4BD4, indicating stack-shape padding was needed for matching.
Corrected 64-bit conversion and timer-related signatures, including __cvt_dbl_usll(double) returning u64 and lb_80019880(u64), to match generated calling conventions.
Preserved nonmatched functions as /// #symbol stubs in the new units so extraction did not require solving every function in the range at once.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
The decomp.dev bot reported matched code at 30.94%, up 0.09% and +3312 bytes for GALE01.
The bot listed 50 new matches, led by gm_801ADE1C in gm_1A4C, gm_801A43A0 in gm_1A3F, gm_801A4BD4 in gm_1A45, gm_801AECC4 in gm_1A4C, and gm_801AE544/gm_801AE44C in gm_1A4C.
The bot also reported 3 improvements in unmatched functions: gm_801AC6D8 to 77.63%, gm_801A4014 to 82.47%, and gm_801AEBB0 to 91.30%, all now under the new split units.

The bot listed many broken/regressed entries in the old gm_1A36 unit; based on the diff, these are primarily functions moved out of gm_1A36 into gm_1A3F/gm_1A45/gm_1A4C rather than evidence of lost source.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename gm_1A3F/gm_1A45/gm_1A4C symbols after this split.
Search for later changes to MinorSceneInfo, MinorSceneHandler OnLoad/OnLeave signatures, or GameState::data.
Look for follow-up PRs that turn gm_1A3F.c, gm_1A45.c, or gm_1A4C.c from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py.
Search for later symbol refinements around gm_803DD4D0 and lbl_803DD4F4 through lbl_803DD53C.
Check whether gm_801A4014, gm_801AC6D8, and gm_801AEBB0 were fully matched after the partial improvements here.
